india_trends.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 12: Kalyan Jewellers, Adani Power, Anant Raj, Tata Motors, BEML, UPL among top losers
india_trends.summary: On May 12, the Indian stock market experienced significant declines as the Nifty 50 fell 1.83% and the Sensex dropped 2%. Investors reacted to fears of an oil shock and ongoing Iran negotiations, leading to bearish trends across all major sectors and rising crude prices.

india_trends.title: Mint Explainer | Why the GST Council plans to review taxation of ride-hailing app companies
india_trends.summary: As ride-hailing companies push for GST exemptions on customer payments, the Goods and Services Tax Council will review existing laws to ensure clarity and uniformity in taxation.

india_trends.title: Bagmane Prime Office REIT IPO: Focus shifts to allotment date. Latest GMP, step-by-step guide to check status
india_trends.summary: The Bagmane Prime Office REIT IPO allotment will be finalised on May 12, with shares credited to demat accounts on May 15. The IPO was oversubscribed 23.71 times, attracting significant investor interest. Refunds for non-allotted shares will begin on May 13.

india_trends.title: Chanel for Chandigarh, Mango for Mangaluru: India’s luxury map shifts beyond metros
india_trends.summary: India's retail landscape is shifting as smaller Tier-2 cities are now attracting global brands and premium malls, outperforming larger metros. A new study reveals a parallel retail economy emerging, driven by strong consumption power and modern infrastructure in these rapidly developing urban center

india_trends.title: LANXESS inks pact with HPCL to sell aviation, industrial lubricants in India, SAARC region
india_trends.summary: Speciality chemicals firm LANXESS has signed an agreement with Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited HPCL to market aviation and industrial lubricants in India and the SAARC region. The partnership aims to provide advanced lubricant solutions.

india_trends.title: India, Russia in advanced talks on critical minerals pact, sources say
india_trends.summary: India and Russia are nearing a critical minerals deal. The agreement will cover exploration, processing, and technology for vital resources like lithium and rare earths. This move aims to reduce India's reliance on China. Corporate investments will also be facilitated.

india_trends.title: Amazon Bazaar clocks 5x growth in 2026 as e-com giant looks to sharpens value commerce play
india_trends.summary: Amazon is boosting its value commerce segment in India. Amazon Bazaar, its low-cost marketplace, saw 5x growth in 2026. The company is integrating Bazaar into the main Amazon app. This move aims to attract shoppers seeking affordable products. Bazaar offers items priced below Rs 300, with a Rs 600 c

india_trends.title: In diversification push, NCDEX eyes equity derivatives debut in 12 months, cash segment by year-end
india_trends.summary: The move will mark one of the most ambitious expansion plans of the exchange since it began operations in 2003 after the Atal Bihari Vajpayee government lifted a four-decade ban on commodity derivatives trading.

india_trends.title: Unique Picks: 6 stocks held by only one MF scheme in April, falling up to 40% in CY26
india_trends.summary: An analysis of mutual fund portfolios revealed 29 stocks held exclusively by single schemes, with most underperforming in CY26. india_trends.title: Hong Kong’s Crown Worldwide plans to double India investments in two years
india_trends.summary: Crown Worldwide Group is set to invest an additional $30 million in India over the next two years. This expansion includes a new integrated storage and information management facility in Coimbatore. The company aims to double its capacity in one of its fastest-growing global markets. This move will

india_trends.title: Multibagger small-cap stock Dynamic Cables plunges 20% after Q4 results
india_trends.summary: Dynamic Cables stock fell 20% on May 12 after a modest Q4 performance, reporting a 3% YoY net profit growth. Despite challenges, the company recorded a 17% revenue increase for FY26 and a strong order book of ₹808 crore, highlighting operational resilience.
